picks.rationale: The Orlando Magic will be without Franz Wagner, a significant offensive contributor, who is listed as out with a calf injury for Game 6. The total for this game has already seen a downward movement of 1-2 points from its opening, with sharp money indicating a belief in a lower-scoring affair. Furthermore, the Magic have seen the total go under in three of their last four playoff games, and both teams have displayed offensive struggles at various points in this tense series. This confluence of injuries, line movement, and recent scoring trends points to a game finishing below the projected total.

The Toronto Raptors have demonstrated strong home-court advantage in this series, winning both Games 3 and 4 outright against the Cavaliers. Cleveland has struggled to win in Toronto this season, holding an 0-3 record including a regular season loss. While Brandon Ingram is questionable for Toronto and Immanuel Quickley is out for the series, the Raptors boast a solid 7-3 record against the spread in their last 10 home playoff games. Given their historical performance at home against Cleveland and their ability to cover the spread in crucial home playoff games, the Raptors are well-positioned to keep this game close or even win outright.

The Houston Rockets enter Game 6 with significant momentum, having won their last two playoff games against the Lakers without Kevin Durant, who is doubtful for tonight's contest. Houston's impressive 30-11 home record this season provides a strong advantage in this elimination game. The Lakers are still without their star Luka Doncic due to a hamstring strain, placing a heavy offensive burden on LeBron James and Austin Reaves. The market has remained steady on Houston as a small home favorite, reflecting confidence in their defensive trajectory and balanced offensive attack to cover the spread.

Cade Cunningham has been the undisputed offensive leader for the Detroit Pistons throughout this series, averaging 32.6 points per game. In Game 5, he delivered a remarkable 45-point performance to keep his team's season alive, highlighting his high usage rate and importance to the Pistons' offense. With key teammates Tobias Harris and Kevin Huerter both listed as questionable, Cunningham's role in generating offense is expected to remain exceptionally high in this pivotal Game 6. His consistent scoring, high volume of shots, and free-throw attempts make him a strong candidate to exceed this points total.

The total for the Cavaliers vs. Raptors game has seen a notable downward adjustment from its opening of 220.5 to the current 218.5, suggesting a belief in a lower-scoring outcome. While the Raptors have gone over the total in four of their last five home games, the Cavaliers have a strong trend of hitting the under in six of their last eight road games. With Brandon Ingram questionable and Immanuel Quickley out for the Raptors, their offensive firepower is diminished, potentially leading to a more defensive struggle. These factors, combined with the line movement, point towards a game that will likely stay under the posted total.

picks.game: Tampa Bay Lightning vs Montreal Canadiens
picks.rationale: The Montreal Canadiens are playing at home in Game 6, holding a 3-2 series lead, and boast a solid 24-15-2 home record. Historically, Montreal has excelled as home underdogs, winning seven of their last eight games in this situation. The Canadiens have also consistently covered the puck line in their last 30 games as underdogs, indicating their ability to keep games close. With the Lightning having lost four of their last six games as favorites, Montreal has a significant situational advantage to close out the series on home ice.

picks.game: Tampa Bay Lightning vs Montreal Canadiens
picks.rationale: A strong trend towards lower-scoring games has emerged in this series, with the last four matchups all finishing with five total goals. Playoff hockey typically features tighter defensive play, and with this being a crucial Game 6, both teams are expected to prioritize limiting scoring chances. While both teams average around 3 goals per game, the heightened stakes often lead to more conservative strategies. This consistent head-to-head trend and the nature of playoff hockey make the Under 5.5 a high-confidence pick.

The Boston Bruins are in a must-win situation at home for Game 6, trailing the series 3-2. They have a strong home record, going 7-3-0 in their last 10 games at TD Garden. The Sabres are dealing with significant injuries, including Noah Ostlund (Out) and Jiri Kulich (Out for Season), with Logan Stanley also day-to-day due to illness. In contrast, the Bruins' only significant injury concern is Viktor Arvidsson being doubtful. The Bruins won their last game against the Sabres 2-1, demonstrating their capability to secure victories in tight contests.

Despite a recent low-scoring head-to-head game, several factors point to a higher-scoring affair in Game 6. The Buffalo Sabres are missing key defensive players like Noah Ostlund and Jiri Kulich, and Logan Stanley is questionable, which could weaken their defensive structure. The Boston Bruins, facing elimination at home, are expected to play aggressively and push their offense. The return of Josh Norris for the Sabres should also provide a boost to their scoring capabilities. This combination of a potentially compromised Sabres defense and an urgent Bruins offense suggests the Over 5.5 is a strong play.

picks.game: Vegas Golden Knights vs Utah Mammoth
picks.rationale: The Vegas Golden Knights enter this Game 5 with strong recent momentum, having won 7 of their last 10 games and currently riding a two-game winning streak. They also secured a 5-4 victory in their most recent head-to-head matchup against the Utah Mammoth on April 27. While William Karlsson remains out for Vegas, the Mammoth are also dealing with an injury to Barrett Hayton, who was recently out. With the series tied 2-2, Vegas's superior recent form and proven ability to win against Utah give them the edge on the road.

The New York Yankees are in excellent form, boasting a 7-3 record in their last 10 games and holding the best pitching staff in MLB, ranking first in fWAR, ERA, xERA, FIP, and xFIP. Conversely, the Baltimore Orioles are severely hampered by injuries to key players, including Ryan Mountcastle (fractured left foot), Jordan Westburg (sprained right UCL), and Jackson Holliday (fractured hamate bone/right hand), all of whom are on the injured list or day-to-day. With the Yankees playing at home and facing a depleted Orioles lineup, they are well-positioned to cover the run line.

The Pittsburgh Pirates' pitching staff has been a major strength this season, ranking tied for second in ERA (3.22) with a dominating starting rotation. Their starters, including Paul Skenes, Carmen Mlodzinski, Braxton Ashcraft, Mitch Keller, and Bubba Chandler, all have ERAs under 3.27. While the Reds have a better overall record, the Pirates are playing at home and have a strong offensive contributor in Oneil Cruz, who is slashing .292/.364/.551 with 6 home runs and 19 RBIs. This strong pitching and home-field advantage give the Pirates an edge.

The Houston Astros swept the Boston Red Sox in their previous series earlier this season, winning 8-1, 9-2, and 6-4. The Red Sox are currently struggling, with a 12-19 record and recently fired manager Alex Cora after a 10-17 start, showing a 2-2 record since the change. Despite the Astros' own somewhat disappointing overall record, their prior dominance over Boston and the Red Sox's current instability make the Astros a valuable pick at plus money.

The Philadelphia Phillies are significantly impacted by injuries, with key players like catcher J.T. Realmuto (lower-back injury), Jhoan Duran (left oblique strain), and Zach Pop (right calf strain) all on the injured list. The Miami Marlins, while having a 15-16 record, have shown some recent form with a 4-6 record in their last ten games, including wins against the Yankees and Braves. Playing at home against a hobbled Phillies team, the Marlins present good value as the underdog.

This game is being played at Coors Field, which is notoriously hitter-friendly and often leads to high-scoring affairs. The Atlanta Braves are dealing with numerous significant injuries to their pitching staff, including Spencer Schwellenbach, AJ Smith-Shawver, Hurston Waldrep, and Spencer Strider, which could weaken their ability to suppress runs. Given the high altitude and the Braves' pitching injuries, the conditions are ripe for a game exceeding the 11-run total.

picks.game: Seattle Sounders FC @ Sporting Kansas City
picks.rationale: The head-to-head history between these two teams strongly favors high-scoring games, with 17 out of 30 (57%) matches going over 2.5 goals and 4 of their last 5 direct matchups also exceeding this total. Sporting Kansas City is in poor form, having failed to win any of their last four home games, and recently suffered a 5-0 loss, suggesting defensive vulnerabilities. Conversely, Seattle Sounders are in good form, having won three away games and scoring consistently. Both teams have shown a propensity for goals in their recent H2H encounters, making the Over 2.5 a confident play.

Real Salt Lake is in strong home form, having won four of their recent home games, indicating a significant home-field advantage. In contrast, Portland Timbers are in poor form, having lost all of their recent matches. While the overall head-to-head record is somewhat even, Real Salt Lake holds a slight edge in recent meetings, winning 16 out of 46 matchups. Given RSL's current excellent home form and Portland's struggles, Real Salt Lake is well-positioned to secure a victory.

The head-to-head history between Atlanta United and CF Montreal shows a tendency for higher-scoring affairs, with 3 of their last 5 matchups going over 2.5 goals. Atlanta United recently broke a five-game winless streak by scoring 2 goals and followed up with a 2-0 win in the US Open Cup, indicating an improved offensive output. CF Montreal, under their interim coach, has also found a scoring touch with back-to-back wins, and their key player Prince Owusu has been prolific, scoring 15 of Montreal's last 35 regular season goals. Both teams are showing signs of offensive improvement, making the Over 2.5 a strong pick.

New England Revolution is in good form and has been unbeatable at Gillette Stadium, suggesting a strong home advantage. In their head-to-head matchups, New England Revolution has a favorable record against Charlotte FC, winning 5 out of 8 games since 2022. New England is also currently ranked higher in the MLS standings at 3rd, compared to Charlotte FC at 5th. These factors combined point towards a confident pick for a New England Revolution victory at home.

The head-to-head history between Philadelphia Union and Nashville SC has seen 4 of their last 5 matches go over 2.5 goals, indicating a trend towards higher-scoring games. Nashville SC is in excellent recent form, winning 67% of their last six outings and currently sitting atop the MLS standings. Both teams have demonstrated a capacity for scoring this season, with Philadelphia averaging 1.79 goals per match and Nashville averaging 1.90 goals per match. This offensive firepower from both sides, coupled with the H2H trend, makes the Over 2.5 a high-confidence pick.
